verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est in una dierum, docente illo populum in templo, et evangelizante, convenerunt principes sacerdotum, et scribæ cum senioribus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ἐν μιᾷ τῶν ἡμερῶν ἐκείνων, διδάσκοντος αὐτοῦ τὸν λαὸν ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ καὶ εὐαγγελιζομένου ἐπέστησαν οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ γραμματεῖς σὺν τοῖς πρεσβυτέροις
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, *that* on one of those days, as he taught the people in the temple, and preached the gospel, the chief priests and the scribes came upon *him* with the elders,

verses.latin_vulgate: et aiunt dicentes ad illum: Dic nobis in qua potestate hæc facis? aut quis est qui dedit tibi hanc potestatem?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἰπὸν πρὸς αὐτόν λέγοντες εἰπε ἡμῖν ἐν ποίᾳ ἐξουσίᾳ ταῦτα ποιεῖς ἢ τίς ἐστιν ὁ δούς σοι τὴν ἐξουσίαν ταύτην
verses.king_james: And spake unto him, saying, Tell us, by what authority doest thou these things? or who is he that gave thee this authority?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Jesus, dixit ad illos: Interrogabo vos et ego unum verbum. Respondete mihi:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτούς ἐρωτήσω ὑμᾶς κἀγὼ ἕνα λόγον καὶ εἴπατέ μοι
verses.king_james: And he answered and said unto them, I will also ask you one thing; and answer me:

verses.latin_vulgate: baptismus Joannis de cælo erat, an ex hominibus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ βάπτισμα ἰωάννου ἐξ οὐρανοῦ ἦν ἢ ἐξ ἀνθρώπων
verses.king_james: The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men?

verses.latin_vulgate: At illi cogitabant intra se, dicentes: Quia si dixerimus: De cælo, dicet: Quare ergo non credidistis illi?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ συνελογίσαντο πρὸς ἑαυτοὺς λέγοντες ὅτι ἐὰν εἴπωμεν ἐξ οὐρανοῦ ἐρεῖ διατί οὖν οὐκ ἐπιστεύσατε αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say, Why then believed ye him not?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem dixerimus: Ex hominibus, plebs universa lapidabit nos: certi sunt enim Joannem prophetam esse.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν δὲ εἴπωμεν ἐξ ἀνθρώπων πᾶς ὁ λαὸς καταλιθάσει ἡμᾶς πεπεισμένος γάρ ἐστιν ἰωάννην προφήτην εἶναι
verses.king_james: But and if we say, Of men; all the people will stone us: for they be persuaded that John was a prophet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et responderunt se nescire unde esset.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπεκρίθησαν μὴ εἰδέναι πόθεν
verses.king_james: And they answered, that they could not tell whence *it was*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Jesus ait illis: Neque ego dico vobis in qua potestate hæc facio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς οὐδὲ ἐγὼ λέγω ὑμῖν ἐν ποίᾳ ἐξουσίᾳ ταῦτα ποιῶ
verses.king_james: And Jesus said unto them, Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cœpit autem dicere ad plebem parabolam hanc: Homo plantavit vineam, et locavit eam colonis: et ipse peregre fuit multis temporibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἤρξατο δὲ πρὸς τὸν λαὸν λέγειν τὴν παραβολὴν ταύτην ἄνθρωπός τις ἐφύτευσεν ἀμπελῶνα καὶ ἐξέδοτο αὐτὸν γεωργοῖς καὶ ἀπεδήμησεν χρόνους ἱκανούς
verses.king_james: Then began he to speak to the people this parable; A certain man planted a vineyard, and let it forth to husbandmen, and went into a far country for a long time.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et in tempore misit ad cultores servum, ut de fructu vineæ darent illi. Qui cæsum dimiserunt eum inanem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐν καιρῷ ἀπέστειλεν πρὸς τοὺς γεωργοὺς δοῦλον ἵνα ἀπὸ τοῦ καρποῦ τοῦ ἀμπελῶνος δῶσιν αὐτῷ οἱ δὲ γεωργοὶ δείραντες αὐτὸν ἐξαπέστειλαν κενόν
verses.king_james: And at the season he sent a servant to the husbandmen, that they should give him of the fruit of the vineyard: but the husbandmen beat him, and sent *him* away empty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et addidit alterum servum mittere. Illi autem hunc quoque cædentes, et afficientes contumelia, dimiserunt inanem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προσέθετο πέμψαι ἕτερον δοῦλον οἱ δὲ κἀκεῖνον δείραντες καὶ ἀτιμάσαντες ἐξαπέστειλαν κενόν
verses.king_james: And again he sent another servant: and they beat him also, and entreated *him* shamefully, and sent *him* away empty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et addidit tertium mittere: qui et illum vulnerantes ejecerunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προσέθετο πέμψαι τρίτον οἱ δὲ καὶ τοῦτον τραυματίσαντες ἐξέβαλον
verses.king_james: And again he sent a third: and they wounded him also, and cast *him* out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem dominus vineæ: Quid faciam? Mittam filium meum dilectum: forsitan, cum hunc viderint, verebuntur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ ὁ κύριος τοῦ ἀμπελῶνος τί ποιήσω πέμψω τὸν υἱόν μου τὸν ἀγαπητόν ἴσως τοῦτον ἰδόντες ἐντραπήσονται
verses.king_james: Then said the lord of the vineyard, What shall I do? I will send my beloved son: it may be they will reverence *him* when they see him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quem cum vidissent coloni, cogitaverunt intra se, dicentes: Hic est hæres, occidamus illum, ut nostra fiat hæreditas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδόντες δὲ αὐτὸν οἱ γεωργοὶ διελογίζοντο πρὸς ἑαυτοὺς, λέγοντες οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ κληρονόμος δεῦτε ἀποκτείνωμεν αὐτόν ἵνα ἡμῶν γένηται ἡ κληρονομία
verses.king_james: But when the husbandmen saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, This is the heir: come, let us kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ejectum illum extra vineam, occiderunt. Quid ergo faciet illis dominus vineæ?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐκβαλόντες αὐτὸν ἔξω τοῦ ἀμπελῶνος ἀπέκτειναν τί οὖν ποιήσει αὐτοῖς ὁ κύριος τοῦ ἀμπελῶνος
verses.king_james: So they cast him out of the vineyard, and killed *him*. What therefore shall the lord of the vineyard do unto them?

verses.latin_vulgate: veniet, et perdet colonos istos, et dabit vineam aliis. Quo audito, dixerunt illi: Absit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλεύσεται καὶ ἀπολέσει τοὺς γεωργοὺς τούτους καὶ δώσει τὸν ἀμπελῶνα ἄλλοις ἀκούσαντες δὲ εἶπον, μὴ γένοιτο
verses.king_james: He shall come and destroy these husbandmen, and shall give the vineyard to others. And when they heard *it*, they said, God forbid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ille autem aspiciens eos, ait: Quid est ergo hoc quod scriptum est: Lapidem quem reprobaverunt ædificantes, hic factus est in caput anguli?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἐμβλέψας αὐτοῖς εἶπεν τί οὖν ἐστιν τὸ γεγραμμένον τοῦτο λίθον ὃν ἀπεδοκίμασαν οἱ οἰκοδομοῦντες οὗτος ἐγενήθη εἰς κεφαλὴν γωνίας
verses.king_james: And he beheld them, and said, What is this then that is written, The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become the head of the corner?

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis qui ceciderit super illum lapidem, conquassabitur: super quem autem ceciderit, comminuet illum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶς ὁ πεσὼν ἐπ᾽ ἐκεῖνον τὸν λίθον συνθλασθήσεται ἐφ᾽ ὃν δ᾽ ἂν πέσῃ λικμήσει αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Whosoever shall fall upon that stone shall be broken; but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quærebant principes sacerdotum et scribæ mittere in illum manus illa hora, et timuerunt populum: cognoverunt enim quod ad ipsos dixerit similitudinem hanc.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐζήτησαν οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ γραμματεῖς ἐπιβαλεῖν ἐπ᾽ αὐτὸν τὰς χεῖρας ἐν αὐτῇ τῇ ὥρᾳ καὶ ἐφοβήθησαν τὸν λαόν ἔγνωσαν γὰρ ὅτι πρὸς αὐτοὺς τὴν παραβολὴν ταύτην εἶπεν
verses.king_james: And the chief priests and the scribes the same hour sought to lay hands on him; and they feared the people: for they perceived that he had spoken this parable against them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et observantes miserunt insidiatores, qui se justos simularent, ut caperent eum in sermone, ut traderent illum principatui, et potestati præsidis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ παρατηρήσαντες ἀπέστειλαν ἐγκαθέτους ὑποκρινομένους ἑαυτοὺς δικαίους εἶναι ἵνα ἐπιλάβωνται αὐτοῦ λόγου εἰς τὸ παραδοῦναι αὐτὸν τῇ ἀρχῇ καὶ τῇ ἐξουσίᾳ τοῦ ἡγεμόνος
verses.king_james: And they watched *him*, and sent forth spies, which should feign themselves just men, that they might take hold of his words, that so they might deliver him unto the power and authority of the governor.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et interrogaverunt eum, dicentes: Magister, scimus quia recte dicis et doces: et non accipis personam, sed viam Dei in veritate doces.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπηρώτησαν αὐτὸν λέγοντες διδάσκαλε οἴδαμεν ὅτι ὀρθῶς λέγεις καὶ διδάσκεις καὶ οὐ λαμβάνεις πρόσωπον ἀλλ᾽ ἐπ᾽ ἀληθείας τὴν ὁδὸν τοῦ θεοῦ διδάσκεις
verses.king_james: And they asked him, saying, Master, we know that thou sayest and teachest rightly, neither acceptest thou the person *of any*, but teachest the way of God truly:

verses.latin_vulgate: Licet nobis tributum dare Cæsari, an non?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔξεστιν ἡμῖν καίσαρι φόρον δοῦναι ἢ οὔ
verses.king_james: Is it lawful for us to give tribute unto Caesar, or no?

verses.latin_vulgate: Considerans autem dolum illorum, dixit ad eos: Quid me tentatis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κατανοήσας δὲ αὐτῶν τὴν πανουργίαν εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτούς τί μέ πειράζετε
verses.king_james: But he perceived their craftiness, and said unto them, Why tempt ye me?

verses.latin_vulgate: ostendite mihi denarium. Cujus habet imaginem et inscriptionem? Respondentes dixerunt ei: Cæsaris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπιδείξατέ μοι δηνάριον τίνος ἔχει εἰκόνα καὶ ἐπιγραφήν ἀποκριθέντες δὲ εἶπον, καίσαρος
verses.king_james: Shew me a penny. Whose image and superscription hath it? They answered and said, Caesar's.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait illis: Reddite ergo quæ sunt Cæsaris, Cæsari: et quæ sunt Dei, Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς, ἀπόδοτε τοίνυν τὰ καίσαρος καίσαρι καὶ τὰ τοῦ θεοῦ τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, Render therefore unto Caesar the things which be Caesar's, and unto God the things which be God's.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non potuerunt verbum ejus reprehendere coram plebe: et mirati in responso ejus, tacuerunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐκ ἴσχυσαν ἐπιλαβέσθαι αὐτοῦ ῥήματος ἐναντίον τοῦ λαοῦ καὶ θαυμάσαντες ἐπὶ τῇ ἀποκρίσει αὐτοῦ ἐσίγησαν
verses.king_james: And they could not take hold of his words before the people: and they marvelled at his answer, and held their peace.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accesserunt autem quidam sadducæorum, qui negant esse resurrectionem, et interrogaverunt eum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσελθόντες δέ τινες τῶν σαδδουκαίων οἱ ἀντιλέγοντες ἀνάστασιν μὴ εἶναι ἐπηρώτησαν αὐτὸν
verses.king_james: Then came to *him* certain of the Sadducees, which deny that there is any resurrection; and they asked him,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicentes: Magister, Moyses scripsit nobis: Si frater alicujus mortuus fuerit habens uxorem, et hic sine liberis fuerit, ut accipiat eam frater ejus uxorem, et suscitet semen fratri suo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγοντες διδάσκαλε μωσῆς ἔγραψεν ἡμῖν ἐάν τινος ἀδελφὸς ἀποθάνῃ ἔχων γυναῖκα καὶ οὗτος ἄτεκνος ἀποθάνῃ ἵνα λάβῃ ὁ ἀδελφὸς αὐτοῦ τὴν γυναῖκα καὶ ἐξαναστήσῃ σπέρμα τῷ ἀδελφῷ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Saying, Master, Moses wrote unto us, If any man's brother die, having a wife, and he die without children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Septem ergo fratres erant: et primus accepit uxorem, et mortuus est sine filiis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἑπτὰ οὖν ἀδελφοὶ ἦσαν καὶ ὁ πρῶτος λαβὼν γυναῖκα ἀπέθανεν ἄτεκνος
verses.king_james: There were therefore seven brethren: and the first took a wife, and died without children.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et sequens accepit illam, et ipse mortuus est sine filio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔλαβεν ὁ δεύτερος τὴν γυναῖκα, καὶ οὑτὸς απέθανεν ἄτεκνος
verses.king_james: And the second took her to wife, and he died childless.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tertius accepit illam. Similiter et omnes septem, et non reliquerunt semen, et mortui sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁ τρίτος ἔλαβεν αὐτήν ὡσαύτως δὲ καὶ οἱ ἑπτὰ οὐ κατέλιπον τέκνα καὶ ἀπέθανον
verses.king_james: And the third took her; and in like manner the seven also: and they left no children, and died.

verses.latin_vulgate: Novissime omnium mortua est et mulier.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὕστερον δὲ πάντων ἀπέθανεν καὶ ἡ γυνὴ
verses.king_james: Last of all the woman died also.

verses.latin_vulgate: In resurrectione ergo, cujus eorum erit uxor? siquidem septem habuerunt eam uxorem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν τῇ οὖν ἀναστάσει τίνος αὐτῶν γίνεται γυνὴ οἱ γὰρ ἑπτὰ ἔσχον αὐτὴν γυναῖκα
verses.king_james: Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them is she? for seven had her to wife.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait illis Jesus: Filii hujus sæculi nubunt, et traduntur ad nuptias:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς οἱ υἱοὶ τοῦ αἰῶνος τούτου γαμοῦσιν καὶ ἐκγαμίσκονται,
verses.king_james: And Jesus answering said unto them, The children of this world marry, and are given in marriage:

verses.latin_vulgate: illi vero qui digni habebuntur sæculo illo, et resurrectione ex mortuis, neque nubent, neque ducent uxores:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ καταξιωθέντες τοῦ αἰῶνος ἐκείνου τυχεῖν καὶ τῆς ἀναστάσεως τῆς ἐκ νεκρῶν οὔτε γαμοῦσιν οὔτε ἐκγαμίσκονται
verses.king_james: But they which shall be accounted worthy to obtain that world, and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry, nor are given in marriage:

verses.latin_vulgate: neque enim ultra mori potuerunt: æquales enim angelis sunt, et filii sunt Dei, cum sint filii resurrectionis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὔτε γὰρ ἀποθανεῖν ἔτι δύνανται ἰσάγγελοι γάρ εἰσιν καὶ υἱοί εἰσιν τοῦ θεοῦ τῆς ἀναστάσεως υἱοὶ ὄντες
verses.king_james: Neither can they die any more: for they are equal unto the angels; and are the children of God, being the children of the resurrection.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia vero resurgant mortui, et Moyses ostendit secus rubum, sicut dicit Dominum, Deum Abraham, et Deum Isaac, et Deum Jacob.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι δὲ ἐγείρονται οἱ νεκροὶ καὶ μωσῆς ἐμήνυσεν ἐπὶ τῆς βάτου ὡς λέγει κύριον τὸν θεὸν ἀβραὰμ καὶ τὸν θεὸν ἰσαὰκ καὶ τόν θεὸν ἰακώβ
verses.king_james: Now that the dead are raised, even Moses shewed at the bush, when he calleth the Lord the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus autem non est mortuorum, sed vivorum: omnes enim vivunt ei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θεὸς δὲ οὐκ ἔστιν νεκρῶν ἀλλὰ ζώντων πάντες γὰρ αὐτῷ ζῶσιν
verses.king_james: For he is not a God of the dead, but of the living: for all live unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondentes autem quidam scribarum, dixerunt ei: Magister, bene dixisti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκριθέντες δέ τινες τῶν γραμματέων εἶπον, διδάσκαλε καλῶς εἶπας
verses.king_james: Then certain of the scribes answering said, Master, thou hast well said.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et amplius non audebant eum quidquam interrogare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκέτι δὲ ἐτόλμων ἐπερωτᾶν αὐτὸν οὐδέν
verses.king_james: And after that they durst not ask him any *question at all*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem ad illos: Quomodo dicunt Christum filium esse David?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ πρὸς αὐτούς πῶς λέγουσιν τὸν χριστὸν υἱόν δαβὶδ εἶναι
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, How say they that Christ is David's son?

verses.latin_vulgate: et ipse David dicit in libro Psalmorum: Dixit Dominus Domino meo: sede a dextris meis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὐτὸς δαβὶδ λέγει ἐν βίβλῳ ψαλμῶν εἶπεν ὁ κύριος τῷ κυρίῳ μου κάθου ἐκ δεξιῶν μου
verses.king_james: And David himself saith in the book of Psalms, The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,

verses.latin_vulgate: donec ponam inimicos tuos scabellum pedum tuorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕως ἂν θῶ τοὺς ἐχθρούς σου ὑποπόδιον τῶν ποδῶν σου
verses.king_james: Till I make thine enemies thy footstool.

verses.latin_vulgate: David ergo Dominum illum vocat: et quomodo filius ejus est?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δαβὶδ οὖν κύριον αὐτὸν καλεῖ καὶ πῶς υἱός αὐτοῦ ἐστιν
verses.king_james: David therefore calleth him Lord, how is he then his son?

verses.latin_vulgate: Audiente autem omni populo, dixit discipulis suis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούοντος δὲ παντὸς τοῦ λαοῦ εἶπεν τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Then in the audience of all the people he said unto his disciples,

verses.latin_vulgate: Attendite a scribis, qui volunt ambulare in stolis, et amant salutationes in foro, et primas cathedras in synagogis, et primos discubitus in conviviis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσέχετε ἀπὸ τῶν γραμματέων τῶν θελόντων περιπατεῖν ἐν στολαῖς καὶ φιλούντων ἀσπασμοὺς ἐν ταῖς ἀγοραῖς καὶ πρωτοκαθεδρίας ἐν ταῖς συναγωγαῖς καὶ πρωτοκλισίας ἐν τοῖς δείπνοις
verses.king_james: Beware of the scribes, which desire to walk in long robes, and love greetings in the markets, and the highest seats in the synagogues, and the chief rooms at feasts;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui devorant domos viduarum, simulantes longam orationem: hi accipient damnationem majorem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἳ κατεσθίουσιν τὰς οἰκίας τῶν χηρῶν καὶ προφάσει μακρὰ προσεύχονται οὗτοι λήψονται περισσότερον κρίμα
verses.king_james: Which devour widows' houses, and for a shew make long prayers: the same shall receive greater damnation.
